name: Qatar
intro: Qatar's economy concentrates an unusual amount of project work into a small market: LNG expansion, infrastructure renewal after the World Cup cycle, and a government pushing digital services aggressively. For project managers and Scrum Masters in Doha, certification is often the line between contract roles and permanent positions with Qatari entities that follow formal HR frameworks. You can join a live online cohort in Qatar year-round, on either a weekend Sat–Sun or a midweek Wed–Thu schedule, both timed for comfortable Qatar daytime rather than awkward hours; exact converted timings for each upcoming date appear in the table below. You train alongside an international cohort — much like the multinational teams that dominate Doha workplaces. Fees are billed in US dollars with invoices Qatari employers accept for sponsorship. Afterwards we help with the exam application, provide mock exams, and advise on how certification is weighted by recruiters serving Qatar's energy and government sectors.

faq.q: How much does certification training cost in Qatar?
faq.a: The course fee is US$1,295 for PMP and US$695 for CSM, covering the full live online weekend workshop, study materials, mock exams and exam application support. PMI's PMP exam fee (US$425 member / US$675 non-member) is paid separately to PMI; the CSM exam is included in the course fee.

faq.q: Are the classes live or recorded?
faq.a: Every batch is taught live by an instructor over two weekend days — not pre-recorded videos. You can ask questions in real time, work through exercises with the cohort, and reach the trainer afterwards for doubts.

faq.q: Is the certification recognised by employers in Qatar?
faq.a: Yes. PMP (from PMI) and CSM (from Scrum Alliance) are global credentials recognised identically everywhere, including by employers and recruiters across Qatar.
